rows.finding_summary: The package previously kept active and superseded revisions in one docs directory, making active-corpus truth depend on a highest-revision inference rather than explicit placement.
rows.canonical_ruling: Exactly one active revision of each controlled document ID shall live in docs/active. Older revisions shall live in docs/archive.

rows.term_cluster: profile / restore / workspace state / machine state
rows.source_docs: UDQ-PROF-SPEC-001 r0; UDQ-UI-MOD-001 r1; UDQ-ARCH-NAR-001 r3; UDQ-GOV-GLO-001 r1
rows.finding_summary: The corpus separated continuity from live truth in spirit, but the subsystem layer still needed stronger operational anti-conflation language.
rows.canonical_ruling: Restore reconstructs local/session/configuration context and shall never by itself imply live machine-state restoration.
rows.resolution_vehicle: UDQ-PROF-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-UI-MOD-001 r2; UDQ-GOV-RPT-002 r0

rows.source_docs: UDQ-OUT-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-UI-SPEC-004 r0; UDQ-REM-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-GOV-GLO-001 r1
rows.finding_summary: The corpus used the terms consistently enough for planning, but the subsystem layer still needed stronger anti-conflation language where overlays and remote presentation occur.
rows.canonical_ruling: Requested, applied, and observed state remain distinct. UI and remote surfaces may visualize them, but shall not redefine or collapse them.
rows.resolution_vehicle: UDQ-OUT-SPEC-001 r2; UDQ-UI-SPEC-004 r1; UDQ-REM-SPEC-001 r2; universalDAQ_term_usage_matrix_r2

rows.term_cluster: live / historical / review mode / live trace
rows.source_docs: UDQ-HIS-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-UI-SPEC-004 r0; UDQ-UI-MOD-001 r1
rows.finding_summary: The four terms were centrally defined, but historian and graph-spec language still needed a tighter operational split.
rows.canonical_ruling: Historical review is evidence exploration, review mode is the broader UI posture, and live trace remains a live explainability surface.
rows.resolution_vehicle: UDQ-HIS-SPEC-001 r2; UDQ-UI-SPEC-004 r1; UDQ-EXM-SPEC-001 r1

rows.term_cluster: rules and sequences versus direct writes
rows.source_docs: UDQ-LOG-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-SEQ-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-OUT-SPEC-001 r1
rows.finding_summary: Rules and sequences were intended to remain governed, but the subsystem layer still needed an explicit statement that they do not bypass arbitration/authorization.
rows.canonical_ruling: Rules and sequences emit governed requests or state transitions and do not become hidden direct-write paths.
rows.resolution_vehicle: UDQ-LOG-SPEC-001 r2; UDQ-SEQ-SPEC-001 r2; UDQ-OUT-SPEC-001 r2

rows.term_cluster: remote capability parity versus backend policy
rows.source_docs: UDQ-REM-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-SEC-SPEC-001 r0; UDQ-UI-SPEC-005 r0
rows.finding_summary: Remote observation and control boundaries were discussed, but the subsystem layer still needed more explicit non-parity and attribution language.
rows.canonical_ruling: Remote clients are policy-bounded capability classes; parity with local capability shall never be assumed.
rows.resolution_vehicle: UDQ-REM-SPEC-001 r2; UDQ-SEC-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-GOV-RPT-002 r0

rows.source_docs: UDQ-SEC-SPEC-001 r0; UDQ-UI-MOD-001 r1; UDQ-OUT-SPEC-001 r1
rows.finding_summary: The corpus stated backend authority clearly, but subsystem wording still needed a direct statement that enabled controls do not equal authorization.
rows.canonical_ruling: Authorization is backend-enforced policy. UI visibility and enabled-state are presentation choices and never constitute authorization by themselves.
rows.resolution_vehicle: UDQ-SEC-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-OUT-SPEC-001 r2; UDQ-UI-MOD-001 r2

rows.finding_summary: The event model already implied evidence retention, but the subsystem docs needed stronger cross-document wording so acknowledgment could not be mistaken for historical erasure.
rows.canonical_ruling: Acknowledgment records recognition and attribution; it does not erase the alarm/event history.
rows.resolution_vehicle: UDQ-EVT-SPEC-001 r1; UDQ-HIS-SPEC-001 r2; UDQ-EXM-SPEC-001 r1
